title: 10 Pcs 8mm Masonry Drill Bits Set, Carbide Concrete Drill Bits for Brick, Stone, Concrete, Tile, Ceramic, Porcelain, Glass, Plastic, Wood and More, Quick Change 6mm Hex Shank, with Sturdy Case
features: Wide Usage - Suitable for drilling into concrete, stone, brick, tile, ceramic, porcelain, granite, marble, glass, soft metal, plastic, wood, and more, providing versatility for various projects.

Easy Bit Changes - The 6mm hex shank design facilitates quick and easy bit changes, fitting all impact and hammer drills.When drilling hard or brittle materials, please disable the hammer mode to prevent damage to both the material and the drill bit.
Durable Construction - Made from high speed steel with cemented carbide bit tips, these drill bits offer premium performance and reliability.
Precise & Clean Drilling - The angled wide flute design ensures smooth and neat holes with minimum bit walking or breaks or chips in a efficient manner.
Maintaining Sharpness - Use water or coolant when drilling harder materials to extend the bit life and maintain sharpness.
description: Enhance your drilling capabilities with the 10 Pcs 8mm Masonry Drill Bits Set. These bits are designed to handle a wide range of drilling tasks in materials such as concrete, brick, tile, ceramic, glass, metal, plastic, and wood. Constructed from durable high-speed steel with cemented carbide bit tip, these bits ensure long-lasting performance. The 6mm hex shank design allows for quick and hassle-free bit changes, compatible with all standard impact and hammer drills. User Guides 1. Use Proper Drill Speed and Pressure: Start drilling at a slow speed to create a pilot hole, then gradually increase to a proper speed as needed. Apply steady and even pressure; avoid excessive force which can bend or break the drill bit. 2. Cooling and Lubrication: When drilling tough materials like concrete or stone, use water or a suitable lubricant to keep the drill bit cool. Overheating can damage the temper of the bit. 3. Avoid Rebar and Hard Obstacles to avoid damaging the bit. 4. Avoid Hammer Function on Non-Impact Bits: If you have non-impact masonry drill bits, avoid using them with a hammer drill setting. This can lead to premature wear and damage to the drill bit. 5. Clean After Use: After drilling, clean your masonry drill bits with a brush to remove dust and debris. This helps maintain their cutting performance and prevents corrosion. 6. Proper Storage: Store your masonry drill bits in a secure and dry place. Avoid storing them where they can rub against each other or against hard surfaces, which can dull the cutting edges.
